vBRIEFInfo.description: Story 3 of #845 triage v1: per-issue triage commands accept|reject|defer|needs-ac|mark-duplicate|status|reset|history. Owner: Agent A3 (wave 2, depends on A1 cache + A2 audit log).

narratives.Problem: Triage decisions need a clear command surface. The reject path must close the upstream GitHub issue with a reason + label and record the decision reversibly.
narratives.Overview: Implement `scripts/triage_actions.py` consuming Story 1 (cache) for issue context and Story 2 (audit log) for decision recording. Exposes Taskfile targets: `triage:accept <N>`, `triage:reject <N> --reason <r>`, `triage:defer <N>`, `triage:needs-ac <N>`, `triage:mark-duplicate <N> --of <M>`, `triage:status <N>` (current decision), `triage:reset <N>` (revert to prior), `triage:history <N>` (decision timeline).
narratives.Constraint: Reject MUST close upstream via `gh issue close <N> --comment '<reason>' --reason 'not planned'` AND apply a `triage-rejected` label. Reset MUST be reversible (writes a `reset` audit entry pointing at the prior decision_id; does NOT delete history). Mark-duplicate MUST validate that the linked-to issue exists in cache. All actions MUST be idempotent on already-final state (re-rejecting a rejected issue is a no-op, not an error).
narratives.Outcome: Each action emits a single audit-log line via Story 2's `append()`. Reject also performs the upstream close. Status/history are read-only against the audit log. Tests cover: each action's happy path + idempotency; reject upstream-close failure recovery (audit entry MUST roll back if `gh issue close` fails); duplicate-of validation; reset chain depth ≥ 2.
narratives.Test: pytest tests/test_triage_actions.py covering: (1) accept records audit entry; (2) reject closes upstream + records + labels (mock `gh`); (3) defer records; (4) needs-ac records + posts AC-request comment to upstream; (5) mark-duplicate validates target; (6) status returns latest; (7) history returns timeline ordered by timestamp; (8) reset writes new entry referencing prior; (9) reject failure rolls back audit entry; (10) idempotent re-action is no-op.
narratives.Action: Implement scripts/triage_actions.py with public function per action: `accept(N, repo)`, `reject(N, repo, reason)`, etc. Wire tasks/triage-actions.yml STANDALONE fragment with one Taskfile target per action. Imports Story 1 (cache.show) and Story 2 (candidates_log.append/latest_decision).

items.title: Reject closes upstream issue + applies triage-rejected label; rollback on gh failure
All actions idempotent on already-final state
tests/test_triage_actions.py covers all 10 cases per Test narrative
CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] entry: feat(triage): action commands (#845 Story 3)
